section.heading: Coast Guard health-care professionals; licensure portability

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content: Notwithstanding any other provision of law regarding the licensure of health-care providers, a health-care professional described in subsection (b) may practice the health profession or professions of the health-care professional at any location in any State, the District of Columbia, or a Commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States, regardless of where such health-care professional or the patient is located, if the practice is within the scope of the authorized Federal duties of such health-care professional.

subsection.heading: Described Individuals.—
subsection.chapeau: A health-care professional described in this subsection is an individual—

subparagraph.content: a member of the Coast Guard;
a civilian employee of the Coast Guard;
a member of the Public Health Service who is assigned to the Coast Guard; or
any other health-care professional credentialed and privileged at a Federal health-care institution or location specially designated by the Secretary; and
has a current license to practice medicine, osteopathic medicine, dentistry, or another health profession; and
is performing authorized duties for the Coast Guard.

subsection.heading: Definitions.—
subsection.content: In this section, the terms “license” and “health-care professional” have the meanings given those terms in section 1094(e) of title 10.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 115–282, title III, § 305(a), Dec. 4, 2018, 132 Stat. 4245.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 508 was renumbered section 2711 of this title.
